adminSettingsDesc: Profile, language, theme, and access details are grouped here so the main pages stay focused on work.
adminHomeDesc: Start here for a quick view of your workload, then move into the dedicated pages to review cases, monitor analytics, manage access, coordinate with colleagues, and keep your profile up to date.
adminHomeCardCasesBody: Open the case queue, search references, and work only the complaints routed to your desk and region coverage.
adminHomeCardAnalyticsBody: Track workload, risk, closure speed, and hotspot trends without crowding the landing page.
adminHomeCardAccessBody: Create users, assign desks and regions, reset passwords, revoke sessions, and manage administrative roles.
adminHomeCardZonesBody: Maintain the official zone model, rename zones, and move regions between them for routing and analytics.
adminHomeCardInboxBody: Send internal instructions, reply to alerts, and keep administrative requests inside the application.
adminHomeCardTrainingBody: Open the training room for guided help on case handling, analytics, messaging, settings, and role-based workflow questions.
adminHomeCardProfileBody: Update your display name, password, and picture, then review your desk and regional coverage.
adminAnalyticsAccessDenied: Analytics are available only to analysts and super admins.
adminAnalyticsAnonymizedCaseReadingBody: Sample anonymized incidents help ground the operational pattern in real complaint narratives.
adminAnalyticsAverageClosureCycleNote: Average days from submission to closure across the cases in this scope.
adminAnalyticsAverageSessionLengthNote: Average time administrators stay active per tracked session.
adminAnalyticsBacklogPressureNote: {{open}} submitted cases remain open in the current scope.
adminAnalyticsClosureRateNote: {{closed}} of {{submitted}} submitted cases are already closed.
adminAnalyticsClosureSignalDetail: The latest closure level sits {{signal}} compared with the recent pattern.
adminAnalyticsComplaintDriversBody: Look at the strongest stakeholder, issue, target, and geographic drivers behind complaint demand.
adminAnalyticsDraftShareNote: {{drafts}} records are still draft-only and not yet submitted.
adminAnalyticsDriverInterpretationBody: These readings help explain where complaint demand is clustering and how quickly new pressure is forming.
adminAnalyticsDriverParagraph1: Issue demand is {{concentration}}. The issue concentration score is {{score}}, which indicates {{hhi}} across the leading complaint themes.
adminAnalyticsDriverParagraph2: Geographic demand is {{concentration}}, led by {{leaders}} within the selected scope.
adminAnalyticsDriverParagraph3: The latest intake level is {{intake}} while submissions are {{submitted}}, indicating whether pressure is building or settling.
adminAnalyticsGeographicHotspotsSubtitle: The leading areas in scope are {{leaders}}.
adminAnalyticsHeroDesc: Review workload, risk, response speed, hotspot trends, and workforce usage through a cleaner analytical reading of live FEMATA data.
adminAnalyticsInsightDeskConcentrationDe: {{share}} of routed work is sitting with the busiest desks.
adminAnalyticsInsightGeographicConcentra: {{share}} of cases sit inside the leading locations in this scope.
adminAnalyticsInsightIssueConcentrationD: {{share}} of issues sit in the top drivers, showing {{concentration}} demand.
adminAnalyticsInsightResponseFrictionDet: Median response begins {{days}} days after first action.
adminAnalyticsInsightSeverityIndexDetail: The current weighted severity profile is {{value}}.
adminAnalyticsInsightSubmissionVolatilit: Recent submission counts are fluctuating around their usual level.
adminAnalyticsIssueFilterPlaceholder: Type issue types separated by commas
adminAnalyticsLatestIntakeSignalDetail: The latest intake level sits {{signal}} compared with the recent pattern.
adminAnalyticsMedianFirstActionNote: Median days before visible case work begins.
adminAnalyticsMedianResponseNote: Median days before a visible response is recorded.
adminAnalyticsMedianSessionLengthNote: Middle session duration across tracked administrative sessions.
adminAnalyticsNarrative1: {{records}} records are in scope, with {{submitted}} submitted cases, a draft share of {{draftShare}}, and a closure rate of {{closureRate}}.
adminAnalyticsNarrative2: Submission momentum is {{submissionMomentum}} while closure momentum is {{closureMomentum}}. Throughput is {{throughputRatio}}, meaning the system is {{throughputState}}.
adminAnalyticsNarrative3: Issue demand is {{issueConcentration}}, led by {{issueLeaders}}. Geographic demand is {{geographicConcentration}}, led by {{geographyLeaders}}.
adminAnalyticsNarrative4: Severity currently sits at {{severityIndex}}, while first action begins after {{firstAction}} days, response after {{response}} days, and average closure after {{closureCycle}} days.
adminAnalyticsNarrative5: Submission variability stands at {{variability}}, with the latest submission level {{submissionSignal}} and closure level {{closureSignal}} against the recent pattern.
adminAnalyticsNarrative6: {{sessions}} tracked sessions currently average {{averageDuration}} hours, producing a workforce intensity reading of {{workloadIntensity}}.
adminAnalyticsNoRankedSessionActivity: No ranked session activity is available for this scope yet.
adminAnalyticsOperationalParagraph1: The current pressure index is {{value}}, combining backlog, severity, variability, and response lag into a single operational strain signal.
adminAnalyticsOperationalParagraph2: Desk distribution shows {{hhi}}, with {{leaders}} carrying the most routed case volume.
adminAnalyticsOperationalParagraph3: The median gap between first action and recorded response is {{days}} days, which helps indicate where workflow friction is appearing.
adminAnalyticsOperationalReadingBody: This layer translates status, severity, desk routing, and response timing into operational signals that managers can act on.
adminAnalyticsOperationalStructureBody: Review how cases are distributed by status, severity, and desk assignment inside the selected scope.
adminAnalyticsPressureIndexNote: Higher values indicate more operational strain in the current case mix.
adminAnalyticsReadinessIndexNote: Higher values indicate stronger closure pace and response readiness.
adminAnalyticsRecordsInScopeNote: {{share}} of these records are already submitted.
adminAnalyticsReportFiltersBody: Choose the reporting window, scope, language, and optional content before generating the printable management report.
adminAnalyticsResponseGapNote: Days between visible first action and the recorded response point.
adminAnalyticsStatisticalBriefingBody: A narrative reading of the current dataset, combining volume, concentration, response timing, and workforce usage.
adminAnalyticsStatusesSubtitle: Current status mix across the selected scope.
adminAnalyticsSubmissionSignalDetail: The latest submission level sits {{signal}} compared with the recent pattern.
adminAnalyticsThroughputRatioNote: Closure volume compared with submitted volume over the recent period.
adminAnalyticsTopActiveUsersSubtitle: Users with the highest tracked session time in the selected scope.
adminAnalyticsTopUserShareNote: Share of tracked time currently held by the most active user.
adminAnalyticsTrackedSessionsNote: Administrative sessions captured for the selected scope and period.
adminAnalyticsTrendClosedCasesSubtitle: Daily closure counts in the recent period.
adminAnalyticsTrendIntelligenceBody: New records are {{created}}, submissions are {{submitted}}, and closures are {{closed}} across the recent series.
adminAnalyticsTrendNewRecordsSubtitle: Daily record creation counts in the recent period.
adminAnalyticsTrendSubmittedCasesSubtitl: Daily submission counts in the recent period.
adminAnalyticsUsageInterpretationBody: This reading combines user session depth, workload intensity, and desk concentration to understand administrative effort.
adminAnalyticsUsageInterpretationTitle: How people are using the system
adminAnalyticsViewDriversCaption: Demand drivers, issue concentration, and geographic hotspots.
adminAnalyticsViewOperationsCaption: Status mix, routing pressure, response lag, and workflow strain.
adminAnalyticsViewSummaryCaption: Topline workload, closure, draft share, and statistical briefing.
adminAnalyticsViewWorkforceCaption: Session duration, user effort, and concentration of administrative activity.
adminAnalyticsWorkforceParagraph1: {{sessions}} tracked sessions currently average {{average}} hours, with a median of {{median}} hours.
adminAnalyticsWorkforceParagraph2: The busiest user currently holds {{share}} of tracked time, while the overall workload intensity reads {{intensity}}.
adminAnalyticsWorkforceParagraph3: Closure activity is {{signal}} against the recent pattern, which helps frame whether staff effort is translating into finished work.
adminAnalyticsWorkforceUsageBody: Review how staff time, session depth, and active-user concentration are distributed in the system.
adminAnalyticsWorkloadIntensityDetail: Combined time depth per engaged case in the current scope.
adminAnalyticsZoneLoadError: Unable to load zone data for analytics.
adminAnalyticsReportExampleIncidentsBody: The examples below are anonymized summaries selected to illustrate recurring patterns without exposing identifying information.
adminAnalyticsReportGenerateError: Unable to generate the printable report.
adminAnalyticsReportOpenError: The printable report could not be opened.
adminAnalyticsReportOpenFromDashboard: Open the printable report from the analytics dashboard.
adminAnalyticsReportReportingPeriodBody: This report covers the period from {{start}} to {{end}}.
adminAnalyticsReportSignatureBody1: This report has been prepared for management and stakeholder use using the current FEMATA analytics dataset.
adminAnalyticsReportSignatureBody2: Prepared and certified by {{name}}, serving as {{role}}, on {{time}}.
adminAnalyticsReportSignatureBody3: The sign-off below confirms the report attribution and the official analyst certification block attached to this export.
adminAnalyticsReportSupplementaryView: A supplementary view of this section also highlights {{summary}}.
adminTrainingDesc: Use the training room when administrators need guided help with case review, inbox work, analytics, access control, settings, and role-based procedures. Michelle handles direct operational guidance, while Melvin joins when a question needs deeper reasoning or comparison.
adminTrainingGuardrailBody: The training room is for operational help. It will not explain source code, schemas, secret keys, or other protected system internals.
adminTrainingPlaceholder: Ask the training room how to use a FEMATA feature...
adminTrainingEnterHint: Press Enter to send. Use Shift + Enter for a new line.
adminTrainingFallbackReply: The training room is ready for your next FEMATA workflow question.
adminTrainingError: The training room could not answer just now.
adminTrainingAgentMichelleSummary: Handles day-to-day operational guidance, quick explanations, and practical next steps inside FEMATA.
adminTrainingAgentMelvinSummary: Steps in for deeper comparisons, diagnosis, prioritization, and structured reasoning when a question needs heavier analysis.
adminTrainingPrompt1: How do I route and update a case correctly?
adminTrainingPrompt2: How do notifications differ from direct messages?
adminTrainingPrompt3: What can I edit in my profile and what is locked?
adminTrainingPrompt4: How do desk and region assignments control visibility?
adminTrainingPrompt5: When should Michelle bring in Melvin?
adminTrainingStarterOnline: Admin training room online. Michelle handles direct operational help, and Melvin joins when deeper reasoning is needed.
adminTrainingStarterIntro: I'm Michelle. Ask me any FEMATA workflow question. If something needs deeper reasoning or comparison work, I'll bring Melvin in and we'll keep the conversation moving.
adminLoginDesc: Sign in before opening the administrative dashboard. This keeps submitted cases, draft references, routing changes, and internal action history restricted to authorized staff only.
adminLoginCard1Body: Submitted cases and draft-only references remain behind admin login.
adminLoginCard2Body: Location changes and reassignments stay visible only to authorized reviewers.
adminLoginCard3Body: A protected admin session is bound to this browser tab and must be re-opened after the tab is closed.
adminLoginFormDesc: Use your administrator username and password to continue.
adminZoneAddRegionError: Unable to add the region to this zone.
adminZoneRemoveRegionError: Unable to remove the region from this zone.
adminZoneUnauthorized: Zone management is available only to authorized administrators.
adminZoneDesc: Maintain the official zone structure used for routing, analytics, and staff coverage without editing user assignments directly.
adminZoneRegionPlaceholder: Type a region to add into this zone
adminZoneNoMatchingRegions: No matching regions are available for this zone.
adminZoneCoverageBody: {{assigned}} of {{total}} regions are currently attached to an official zone.
adminAccessCreateError: Unable to create the administrator account.
adminAccessUpdateError: Unable to save the administrator changes.
adminAccessRevokeError: Unable to revoke sessions for this administrator.
adminAccessResetError: Unable to reset the password for this administrator.
adminAccessActionError: Unable to complete that access-control action.
adminAccessUnauthorized: Access control is available only to administrators with user-management permission.
adminAccessDesc: Register administrators, assign desks and regions, reset passwords, revoke sessions, and keep formal reporting attributes aligned with each account.
adminAccessSearchPlaceholder: Search by name, username, role, region, or organization
adminProfileDesc: Update your editable account details, refresh your picture and signature, and review the role, desk, and regional coverage attached to this account.
adminProfileMunicipalCoveragePending: Municipal coverage will appear from the current regional assignment.
adminProfileMunicipalsDerived: Municipal coverage is derived from the assigned regions.
adminInboxLoadError: Unable to load messages and notifications.
adminInboxEditError: Unable to save the edited message.
adminInboxDesc: Send direct messages, share attachments, reply to notifications, and keep internal administrative instructions inside FEMATA.
adminInboxNotificationRecipientPlacehold: Choose who should receive the notification
adminInboxStartConversationBody: Narrow the directory by region, municipality, role, and person search before choosing who to message.
adminInboxPersonPlaceholder: Search by person, email, role, or region
adminInboxNoDirectoryMatches: No administrators match the current filters.
adminInboxDirectoryHint: Choose a region, municipality, role, or person search to reveal matching administrators.
adminInboxNoThreads: No message threads are available yet.
adminInboxChooseAdminFirst: Choose an administrator before sending a message.
adminInboxAllAdministratorsNote: Send a notification to every visible administrator account.
adminInboxNotificationResponseBody: You are responding directly to the notification titled “{{title}}”.
adminInboxNoMessages: No messages in this thread yet.
adminInboxMessageHistoryHint: The message pane scrolls internally so the thread stays stable while you write.
adminInboxSubjectLockedHint: This subject is locked because it is tied to a notification reply.
adminInboxResponsePlaceholder: Write your response to this notification...
adminInboxNoConversationSelected: Choose a thread or start a new conversation to open the message workspace.
adminInboxNoOtherAdmins: No other administrators are available for messaging right now.
adminCasesSaveError: Unable to save the case update.
adminCasesAccessDenied: Cases review is available only to users with case visibility.
adminCasesHeroDesc: Review only the cases routed to your desk and regional coverage, then move between queue work, rapid lookup, filters, and case actions without crowding the page.
adminCasesControlTitle: Switch the case surface you need
adminCasesSurfaceQueueDetail: Work through the filtered queue and open a case into the workspace.
adminCasesSurfaceLookupDetail: Search internal tracking or public reference numbers for immediate access.
adminCasesSurfaceFiltersDetail: Narrow by geography and assignment coverage without cluttering the queue.
adminCasesSearchPlaceholder: Search by tracking number, reference, title, target, region, or theme
adminCasesAllStatusesNote: Show every workflow status in the current scope.
adminCasesAllDesksNote: Show every desk that appears in the visible case list.
adminCasesSortRecentNote: Show the most recently updated cases first.
adminCasesSortOldestNote: Show the least recently updated cases first.
adminCasesSortOpenFirstNote: Move closed cases lower in the queue.
adminCasesSortSubmittedFirstNote: Prioritize submitted cases ahead of draft-only references.
adminCasesQueueSummary: Showing {{visible}} of {{total}} matching records. Open a case from the queue to reveal the review workspace on the right.
adminCasesNoQueuedMatches: No queued cases match the current filters.
adminCasesRapidLookupBody: When a caller provides a public reference or internal tracking number, open the case directly without searching the full queue.
adminCasesLookupMissing: Enter an internal tracking number or public reference number first.
adminCasesLookupNoMatch: No matching case was found for that search.
adminCasesNoLiveMatches: No live lookup matches were found.
adminCasesLookupHint: Start typing a tracking number or public reference to reveal live matches.
adminCasesNationwideHint: Leave region on all to keep a nationwide view inside your allowed access scope.
adminCasesMunicipalityHint: Choose a municipality only when you need a narrower regional view.
adminCasesFocusBody: Open one case at a time and use the tabs below to review the overview, public side, activity history, and update actions.
adminCasesPublicAccessActiveUntilClosed: Active until the case is closed.
adminCasesNoPublicNotes: No public notes have been submitted.
adminCasesNoActivity: No activity history has been recorded yet.
adminCasesReadOnlyActions: You can review this case, but your account does not have permission to update it.
adminCasesSuggestedFlow1: Confirm the current status and desk assignment before saving.
adminCasesSuggestedFlow2: Add feedback that is suitable for internal review and future public handling.
adminCasesSuggestedFlow3: Mark action started only when visible work on the case has actually begun.
adminWizardZonePending: Zone will appear after region selection
adminWizardRoleSuperAdminNote: Full system control across users, analytics, zones, messaging, and case review.
adminWizardRoleCaseManagerNote: Works operational cases, routing, updates, and desk-level follow-up.
adminWizardRoleReviewerNote: Reviews routed cases, feedback, and case progression within assigned coverage.
adminWizardRoleAnalystNote: Accesses analytics, reporting exports, and formal analytical sign-off functions.
adminWizardValidationChooseRegion: Choose a region before adding the assignment.
adminWizardValidationDuplicateAssignment: That desk and location assignment already exists.
adminWizardValidationCoverage: Add at least one coverage assignment for this account.
adminWizardValidationPassword: Temporary password must be at least 8 characters.
adminWizardValidationAnalystTitle: Role title is required for analysts.
adminWizardReportingLinePlaceholder: Who does this account report to?
adminWizardZoneAppearsAfterRegion: Zone appears automatically after a region is chosen.
adminWizardAssignmentsBody: Each assignment controls which desk, region, and municipality scope this account can see.
adminWizardAssignmentRequired: Coverage is required for this account.
adminWizardAssignmentOptional: This account can be saved without scoped coverage.
adminWizardAnalystEnabled: Analyst functions are enabled for this account.
adminWizardAnalystDisabled: Analyst functions are not enabled for this account.
adminWizardReviewCreateNote: Review all details before creating the account.
adminWizardReviewEditNote: Review all details before saving the changes.
adminLocalesUnauthorized: Locale management is available only to authorized administrators.
adminLocalesDesc: Enable or disable language bundles for the public interface. Disabled languages will not appear in the language picker.
adminLocalesEnabledHint: Enabled languages appear in the public language picker.
adminLocalesNote1: Swahili (sw) and English (en) are always enabled and cannot be disabled.
adminLocalesNote2: Changes take effect immediately for new users; existing users may need to refresh.
adminLocalesNote3: Disabled languages remain in the system but won't appear in the language picker.
